body.title: ClaudeAndAkienWorkshop — evolve ClaudeAndAkien into multi-project workshop with shared utility closet substrate
body.result: Closed 2026-05-01 — folded into D-claudeandakien-folded-2026-05-01. ClaudeAndAkienWorkshop concept obsolete; all workshop-bound concepts now agent_datacenter material. Surviving scope absorbed by T-skills-master-home-in-datacenter (skills home) and T-capability-extraction-from-igor (substrate migration including cc_skills/ + utility_closet/).
body.description:
## Scope refresh (2026-05-01)

Per Akien: this ticket is now about **getting all the docs about skills + tooling into agent_datacenter**. Three faces:
1. **Classroom** — how the skills work, how to learn them
2. **Practical workshop** — actual tooling/templates Igor and other agents pick up and use
3. **Foundation for all agents** — substrate that any agent inherits when it plugs in

## Install-flow vision (Akien 2026-05-01)

End-state UX:
- Someone downloads the Igor repo
- Runs the platform-specific Igor executable script
- Script sees no datacenter → asks where to put it → fetches the datacenter repo
- Datacenter installs itself, then asks Igor shim to install + run via the shim's install method
- Each component interacts intelligently *even before installation*, summoning help it needs

This makes the workshop the substrate every component bootstraps from.

## Original scope (preserved)

Akien 2026-04-20: segment all utility closet and Claude Code tooling out to an evolution of /home/akien/dev/src/ClaudeAndAkien. Rename: ClaudeAndAkienWorkshop. Role: general-purpose workshop across many projects.

Igor will live and work there too — the utility closet substrate (UC rack, inference channels, comms transports, skills, shared scripts) is what centers every project.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/dev/src/ClaudeAndAkienWorkshop/ (new repo evolution); cross-cuts to wild_igor/ (Igor migration), /home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter/ (substrate alignment).

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (each skill carries its own teaching docstring); test-plan-or-why-not (workshop tooling needs reproducibility tests).

**Scope boundary:** IN — docs ingestion to agent_datacenter, install-flow design, workshop role split (classroom/workshop/foundation). OUT — actual classroom curriculum content, C&A history rewrite, multi-org federation.

**Test plan:** install-flow E2E on a clean machine — repo download → executable → DC install → Igor install → first turn. Each phase verifiable without the next.

## Cross-project lab framing (Akien 2026-05-01 architectural North Star)

The split crystallizes:
- **`agent_datacenter/`** = the cross-project lab (substrate for any agent). The agent-as-pieces. Akien's general-purpose workshop across many projects.
- **`TheIgors/lab/`** = Igor-only lab. Cognition-specific work, design docs, and Igor-internal tooling. NOT cross-project utilities.
- **`/home/akien/dev/src/ClaudeAndAkienWorkshop/`** (this ticket's evolution target) = the workshop tooling and skills Akien uses with CC; sits alongside the datacenter as cross-project material.

This ticket's scope therefore is: **migrate the cross-project tooling currently in `TheIgors/lab/` into the workshop**, while keeping Igor-specific items behind. Examples to discuss at sprint-time design call: the various skills (savestate, day-close, audit, etc.), the audit tooling that's not Igor-specific, palace_sync.py if it's reusable for other projects, channel.py shim, etc.

The install-flow vision (download repo → executable → DC install → Igor install) is the *runtime* manifestation of this split: the datacenter installs first as the substrate, then Igor (or any other agent) installs on top. Every agent that plugs into the workshop inherits the substrate for free.

## Reorg rubric (Akien 2026-05-01)

The categorization rules for the four-tree reorg (TheIgors / TheIgorsProject / .TheIgors / .agent_datacenter), captured durably so any Opus / future-CC pass references the same rubric:

| Source | Destination |
|---|---|
| History (older session/decision/audit logs no longer load-bearing) | TheIgorsProject (for later organization) |
| Tools NOT Igor-specific (cross-project utility shape) | agent_datacenter |
| Older scaffolding for agent_datacenter no longer needed | TheIgorsProject as history, OR delete |
| Tools for doing things *specifically* with Igor (current) | TheIgors/lab (stays) |
| `design_docs/` and `design_docs_for_igor/` | stay in TheIgors (with exceptions below) |
| `design_docs/archive/` (and similar archive subdirs) | TheIgorsProject |
| `design_docs/audit_2026/` | move to TheIgors/lab (it's working-tooling, not human-facing reference) |
| Design docs that are about agent_datacenter capabilities (e.g. `datacenter-swarm-bus-design-2026-05-01.md` written today) | agent_datacenter |
| `.db` files (per no-sqlite rule) | DELETE all of them |

**Underlying principle:** `design_docs/` is for *what a human needs to know to understand the project*. Not every design decision belongs in human-facing reference docs — implementation-specific design lives near the code it shapes. Capability-specific design lives near the capability.

**Trees in scope of the reorg:**
- `/home/akien/TheIgors` (this repo)
- `/home/akien/TheIgorsProject` (project archive / history)
- `/home/akien/.TheIgors` (runtime state for the Igor instance)
- `/home/akien/.agent_datacenter` (runtime state for the datacenter)
- `/home/akien/dev/src/agent_datacenter` (datacenter source repo — implied by "tools not igor-specific" rule and the design-doc relocation)
